answers: Taiwan [or Republic of China or ROC; do not accept or prompt on just “China”]
answers_sanitized: Taiwan [or Republic of China or ROC; do not accept or prompt on just "China"]
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: Tsai Ming-Liang's film Goodbye Dragon Inn includes a five minute shot of one of these empty locations after it closes down for the final time. For 10 points each:
parts_sanitized: Name this type of location. The protagonist of Buster Keaton's Sherlock Jr works at one of these locations.
The characters in Tsai's film are saying goodbye to Dragon Inn, a film by this wuxia[woo-shyah] director who also made A Touch of Zen.
Dragon Inn and Goodbye Dragon Inn are both films from this country. Other directors from this country include Edward Yang and the director of Crouching Tiger, Hidden Dragon, Ang Lee.

bonuses.leadin: A director from this country cast more than a hundred amateur actors in a movie about a murder on Guling Street, and depicted the life of a family led by NJ in another movie. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this setting of A Brighter Summer Day and Yi Yi. A director from this country made A City of Sadness and The Assassin, which was screened at Cannes in 2015.
Another Taiwanese director besides Edward Yang and Hou Hsiao-Hsien is Ang Lee, who adapted this film about the relationship between two cowboys in Wyoming from an Annie Proulx story.
Inexplicably, Brokeback Mountain lost the 2005 Best Picture Oscar to this film about racial tensions in Los Angeles. David Cronenberg adapted a J. G. Ballard novel of the same name.
